mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect expanding manufacturing activity and supply-side momentum, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO monthly growth is positive but the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below a threshold (e.g., 6.5%) signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.5% and remains below for at least 3 months
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE is below 6.5% for 3 months, if the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns is not significantly greater than 50% (one-sided binomial test p > 0.05), reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion from both residential investment and consumption, which together drive S&P 500 earnings higher over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST 3-month moving average and RSAFS 3-month moving average are higher than 6 months prior
failure_condition: If the joint condition is met but the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 60% of cases, or if the interaction term in a regression of returns on HOUST and RSAFS individually is not statistically significant (p>0.10), the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI YoY < 2%), low and stable federal funds rate supports equity valuations by reducing discount rates and signaling accommodative policy, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during periods with CPI < 2% and FEDFUNDS < 2%, the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is not positive more than 60% of the time over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth provides the most comprehensive measure of economic expansion; positive GDP growth signals rising aggregate demand and corporate profits, which leads to higher S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If real GDP growth is positive but the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of quarters over a 10-year period, the hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate from ultra-low levels (e.g., crossing 0.25%) signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle that increases the cost of capital and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25%, if the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of cases over a 2-year period, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in payroll employment (PAYEMS rising) and reduction in unemployment (UNRATE falling) over a quarter indicates a robust labor market that boosts consumer income and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change positive and UNRATE 3-month change negative
failure_condition: If the joint condition is met but the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of cases, or if the interaction effect in a linear model is not positive and significant (p<0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: During industrial expansion phases (INDPRO rising month-over-month), positive momentum in manufacturing carries over to short-term equity markets, with rising INDPRO predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO MoM is positive but the forward 5-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 55% of cases over a 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.
